description: A frame or machine for weaving cloth; the structure that holds threads for interlacing. 'Weloa' names what enables weaving—the frame where warp meets weft.
sound_symbolism: The word weaves complete. 'We' interlaces in relationship, threads crossing. 'Lo' opens the frame wide. Then 'a' completes with open vowel, cloth emerging.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo weloa' (the loom). Creates 'wetha' (cloth) from 'sima' (thread). Center of textile production.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The loom as heart of home—where cloth for family was made thread by thread.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Pattern emerging—the loom reveals design through repetition, mathematics made visible.
